texto: I say I write to you, but, truth be told, I prefer the American idiom: I write you. I'll write you friends say, as if, in writing, someone could be caused to appear, as if writing were a spell, some form of conjuration. So, I write you to bring you here, bring you back.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Julian Wolfreys: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I say I write to you, but, truth be told, I prefer the American idiom: I write you. I'll write yo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, magic, writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e writing.

frase_original: I say I write to you, but, truth be told, I prefer the American idiom: I write you. I'll write you friends say, as if, in writing, someone could be caused to appear, as if writing were a spell, some form of conjuration. So, I write you to bring you here, bring you back.
